channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Oil surges to its highest price since 2023, and stocks drop after U.S. jobs report
item.description: Oil shot to its highest price since 2023 after surging again because of the Iran war, and a weak update on the U.S. job market knocked stocks lower to cap Wall Street's worst week since October.
item.content:encoded:
Oil shot to its highest price since 2023 after surging again because of the Iran war, and a weak update on the U.S. job market knocked stocks lower to cap Wall Street's worst week since October.

(Image credit: Seth Wenig)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: No lawsuits required: U.S. Customs is working on a system to refund tariffs
item.description: U.S. Customs told the trade court it aims for a streamlined process in 45 days to return importers' money without requiring individual lawsuits.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. Customs told the trade court it aims for a streamlined process in 45 days to return importers' money without requiring individual lawsuits.

(Image credit: Ted S. Warren)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: The U.S. unexpectedly loses 92,000 jobs, adding to worries about the economy
item.description: The job market showed further signs of weakness last month as employers cut 92,000 jobs. The unemployment rate inched up to 4.4%, from 4.3% in January.
item.content:encoded:
The job market showed further signs of weakness last month as employers cut 92,000 jobs. The unemployment rate inched up to 4.4%, from 4.3% in January.

(Image credit: Spencer Platt)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: 2 young billionaires are behind the prediction market boom. They hate each other
item.description: The 20-something billionaires who run Kalshi and Polymarket are battling it out to be the top prediction market company. Observers and former insiders say the feud is just heating up.
item.content:encoded: The 20-something billionaires who run Kalshi and Polymarket are battling it out to be the top prediction market company. Observers and former insiders say the feud is just heating up.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: U.S. businesses that paid emergency tariffs are now trying to recover their funds
item.description: Businesses that paid more than 100-billion dollars in tariffs are waiting to find out when they'll get their money back, after the Supreme Court ruled those import taxes were illegal.
item.content:encoded: Businesses that paid more than 100-billion dollars in tariffs are waiting to find out when they'll get their money back, after the Supreme Court ruled those import taxes were illegal.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Wall Street is betting on tariff refunds after Supreme Court ruling
item.description: When the Supreme Court struck down many of President Trump's tariffs, it left importers wondering how long they'd have to wait to get their money back. Hedge funds are offering to help out.
item.content:encoded:
When the Supreme Court struck down many of President Trump's tariffs, it left importers wondering how long they'd have to wait to get their money back. Hedge funds are offering to help out.

(Image credit: Spencer Platt)
item.dc:creator: Vito Emanuel

item.title: WATCH: How traffic dried up in the Strait of Hormuz since the Iran war began
item.description: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz is "about as wrong as things could go" for global oil markets. Iran achieved it not with a naval blockade, but with cheap drones.
item.content:encoded: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz is "about as wrong as things could go" for global oil markets. Iran achieved it not with a naval blockade, but with cheap drones.
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: A Biden-era rule sought to stabilize child care. Why Trump wants it gone
item.description: The Trump administration has proposed repealing a Biden-era rule that required states to change how they pay out child care subsidies, citing the potential for fraud.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration has proposed repealing a Biden-era rule that required states to change how they pay out child care subsidies, citing the potential for fraud.

(Image credit: Nadezhda1906)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: America has a housing affordability crisis. Building houses for rent can help
item.description: Developers are building more single-family houses for renting. That can lower prices for both renters and buyers.
item.content:encoded:
Developers are building more single-family houses for renting. That can lower prices for both renters and buyers.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: An Arkansas manufacturer pins hope on apprenticeships
item.description: Virco Manufacturing has made school furniture since 1950. Now with competition from imports and an aging workforce, Virco is hoping to build the workforce it needs to survive through apprenticeships.
item.content:encoded: Virco Manufacturing has made school furniture since 1950. Now with competition from imports and an aging workforce, Virco is hoping to build the workforce it needs to survive through apprenticeships.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu
